examples.title: Barcode Reader Data Processing Algorithm
examples.description: Demonstrates how a barcode reader processes input data
examples.code:
ALGORITHM: BarcodeReader
BEGIN
 INPUT: ReflectedLightPattern[]
 
 // Step 1: Acquire analog signal
 AnalogSignal <- ReadPhotoSensor()
 
 // Step 2: Convert to digital
 DigitalSignal <- ADC(AnalogSignal)
 
 // Step 3: Edge detection
 Edges[] <- DetectEdges(DigitalSignal)
 
 // Step 4: Measure bar/space widths
 Widths[] <- MeasureWidths(Edges)
 
 // Step 5: Decode using encoding scheme
 DecodedData <- DecodeBarcode(Widths)
 
 // Step 6: Verify checksum
 IF ValidateChecksum(DecodedData) THEN
 Output <- DecodedData
 ELSE
 Output <- "ERROR"
 ENDIF
 
 RETURN Output
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm shows the step-by-step process of how a barcode reader converts reflected light patterns into usable data, including signal acquisition, digitization, edge detection, width measurement, and checksum validation.

examples.title: Keyboard Key Detection Process
examples.description: Explains how keyboard detects and reports key presses
examples.code:
ALGORITHM: KeyboardScan
BEGIN
 INITIALIZE KeyMatrix[Rows][Cols]
 INITIALIZE KeyState[TotalKeys]
 
 PROCEDURE ScanKeyboard()
 FOR each Row IN KeyMatrix DO
 SetRowHigh(Row) // Apply voltage to row
 
 FOR each Col IN KeyMatrix DO
 IF ReadColumn(Col) == HIGH THEN
 KeyCode <- GetKeyCode(Row, Col)
 IF KeyState[KeyCode] == RELEASED THEN
 KeyState[KeyCode] <- PRESSED
 SendToComputer(KeyCode, PRESSED)
 ENDIF
 ELSE
 IF KeyState[KeyCode] == PRESSED THEN
 KeyState[KeyCode] <- RELEASED
 SendToComputer(KeyCode, RELEASED)
 ENDIF
 ENDIF
 ENDFOR
 
 SetRowLow(Row) // Remove voltage
 ENDFOR
 ENDPROCEDURE
 
 WHILE SystemActive DO
 ScanKeyboard()
 ENDWHILE
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the matrix scanning technique used in keyboards to detect which key is pressed. The keyboard controller scans rows and columns to identify the exact key pressed, then sends the corresponding scan code to the computer.

examples.title: Optical Mouse Movement Detection
examples.description: Shows how an optical mouse calculates movement
examples.code:
ALGORITHM: OpticalMouseTracking
BEGIN
 CONSTANT Resolution = 400 // DPI
 INITIALIZE PreviousFrame, CurrentFrame
 INITIALIZE DeltaX, DeltaY
 
 PROCEDURE CaptureFrame()
 // Sensor captures surface image at high frequency
 CurrentFrame <- ReadImageSensor()
 RETURN CurrentFrame
 ENDPROCEDURE
 
 PROCEDURE CalculateMovement()
 // Digital Signal Processor compares frames
 Offset <- CrossCorrelate(PreviousFrame, CurrentFrame)
 
 // Convert pixel offset to physical movement
 DeltaX <- Offset.X / Resolution
 DeltaY <- Offset.Y / Resolution
 
 // Send movement data to computer
 SendUSBReport(DeltaX, DeltaY)
 
 // Update previous frame
 PreviousFrame <- CurrentFrame
 ENDPROCEDURE
 
 WHILE MouseActive DO
 CurrentFrame <- CaptureFrame()
 IF FrameValid(CurrentFrame) THEN
 CalculateMovement()
 ENDIF
 ENDWHILE
END
examples.explanation: The optical mouse uses a tiny camera to capture successive images of the surface. The DSP compares these images using cross-correlation to determine how much the mouse has moved, then converts this to cursor movement data sent via USB.
